rag_rules: Insert AGREE annex blocks into the target component implementation scope after subcomponents and connections
Preserve existing AADL architecture by only adding annex blocks, not modifying other elements
Use only visible identifiers in contracts when no explicit whitelist is provided
Place assumptions and guarantees in the appropriate scope based on whether they reference ports or subcomponents
generic_rules: Maintain architectural integrity by only adding annex blocks to the target component or implementation
Preserve existing connections and subcomponents when inserting AGREE annex blocks
Use only visible identifiers in contracts when no explicit whitelist is provided
Place assumptions and guarantees in the appropriate scope based on whether they reference ports or subcomponents
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks for AGREE contracts
Place component type annexes in the component type scope
Place implementation-level bindings in the implementation scope after subcomponents and connections
Use initialized temporal recurrence for stateful equations, e.g., `0.0 -> pre(t) + step`
anti_patterns: Do not use implementation-only subcomponent paths in a component type annex
Do not place assumptions or guarantees in scopes where the referenced features are not visible
Do not modify existing AADL architecture when inserting AGREE annex blocks
Do not use opaque Boolean data range comparisons unless the requirement and AADL data representation provide a numeric scalar
